danger_signals.visual_form: Monsters emerging from corrupted environments; creatures named after unfavorable conditions and seven deadly sins
danger_signals.meaning: Active supernatural threat; world afflicted by specific malevolent forces

danger_signals.visual_form: Dungeons and corrupted landscapes; underworld prison structures (Gratis); mechanically twisted environments
danger_signals.meaning: Areas under demonic corruption; unsafe territories requiring cleansing

danger_signals.visual_form: Tanzra's extraordinary powerful barrier blocking conventional descent; Tower of Babel/Tower of Souls as final staged obstacle
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate threat; world-state locked in final conflict; conventional approaches negated

danger_signals.visual_form: King Kolunikus depicted as corrupted by 'Doom' demon; unjust taxation and imprisonment
danger_signals.meaning: Political authority compromised; civilian suffering; kingdom under demonic influence

safety_signals.visual_form: Master's floating palace; heavenly domain serving as base of operations
safety_signals.meaning: Divine sanctuary; source of power and refuge; legitimacy of Master's authority

safety_signals.visual_form: Angelic associates accompanying the Master; visual representation of heavenly forces
safety_signals.meaning: Divine support system; protective presence; moral authority reinforcement

safety_signals.visual_form: Functional wings; sword and shield equipment; magic capabilities
safety_signals.meaning: Player empowered; equipped for conflict; divine intervention made manifest

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sky Castle; statue imagery; sword and wing iconography
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sky Castle domain; heavenly aesthetic; areas restored to order after Master's intervention

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Seven deadly sins embodied as distinct demons; Tower of Babel/Tower of Souls; mechanical god Destruction representing Pride
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Monsters spawned from lesser demons (unfavorable conditions) vs. primary evils (deadly sins); escalating visual complexity and malformation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underground prisons; corrupted cities; mechanically engineered abominations; Death Heim underworld; frozen lake wastes

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: King Kolunikus as visual manifestation of demonic influence on governance
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unjustly imprisoned townspeople vs. corrupted authority figures
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underground prison of Gratis (Leon city); taxation oppression visualized through captivity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple acts per area; lesser demons spawning monsters from unfavorable conditions; cities afflicted by specific demonic forces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Citizens suffering; townspeople imprisoned; rulers corrupted; monsters actively spawning and attacking

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Progressive cleansing (defeating first six deadly sins)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progression through stages; each sin-demon defeated removes one layer of corruption; dungeons cleared
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player becomes stronger; magic becomes more varied; environments shift from hostile to traversable

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final conflict state (Tower of Souls emergence)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tower of Babel appears; mechanically engineered final sin (Destruction/Pride) revealed; environmental shift to ultimate challenge
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monsters require significantly more damage; difficulty escalates; final barrier blocks conventional descent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Underworld prison appears; Tanzra revealed frozen in ice lake; Sky Castle crash-landing visualizes power transfer; angels suffer casualties
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Conventional approaches negated; Sky Castle becomes battering ram; final resurrection of seven sins; ultimate confrontation scenario

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Master's statue slowly eroding; sword and right wing falling off; civilization growth visualized through monument decay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: World becomes self-sufficient; humanity forgetting the Master; independence replacing divine reliance

environmental_storytelling.example: City of Leon under 'Doom' demon; King Kolunikus imposes unjust taxation through demonic influence; underground prison Gratis (free/no one enters free) contrasts with imprisonment
Tower of Babel/Tower of Souls references divine judgment; Tanzra frozen in ice lake parallels Satan in Dante's Inferno; seven deadly sins structure mirrors Divine Comedy cosmology
Dungeons and landscapes visually degrade with each deadly sin's influence; mechanical god Destruction represents industrialized corruption (Pride); underworld Death Heim contrasts with Sky Castle
Tanzra's backstory as fallen servant-turned-rebel visualized through his banishment to underworld; seven sins fueled by hatred; power transfer through wounded angel shows mercy overcoming malice

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Floats from Sky Castle; descends to world below to fight monsters; fully winged and armed
Navigates through dungeons via jumping, flying, falling, floating to platforms; charges magic by holding button; releases magic in forms dependent on position; magic consumption limited per area
Creates extraordinary powerful barrier; resides in Death Heim; leads seven sins in rebellion; manifests as beast frozen waist-deep in ice lake
Spawn heightened challenge monsters; possess greater power than lesser demons; require significantly more damage to destroy; resurrect for final confrontation
Named after conditions (Doom, etc.); spawn initial monster waves; possess reduced power compared to deadly sins; represent entry-level corruption
Imposes unjust taxation; imprisons townspeople in Gratis underground prison; acts as demonic puppet ruler; visual manifestation of Doom demon's influence
Accompany Master; suffer casualties during Sky Castle crash; one wounded angel transfers final power vestige to player before death

notes: ActRaiser 2 employs mythological environmental storytelling grounded in Divine Comedy and Paradise Lost cosmology. Danger signals manifest through demonic corruption hierarchy (lesser demons → seven deadly sins → Tanzra/Pride) visualized progressively through environment and enemy design. Safety signals center on Sky Castle imagery and angelic presence representing divine legitimacy. Political situations communicated through corrupted leadership (King Kolunikus) and civic suffering (imprisoned townspeople), with corruption visualized through environmental imprisonment and unjust governance. World state transitions marked by clearing corrupted areas, tower emergence, underworld breach, and final battle consequences shown through monument erosion in ending. The game visually communicates that civilization's growth depends on destroying demonic influence and that humanity eventually becomes self-sufficient, forgetting divine reliance.
